error.signature: ROS2 bag recording timestamps don't match message timestamps when using sim_time

verdict.summary: ros2 bag record uses wall clock for recording timestamps by default, even when nodes use sim_time. Messages are recorded with wall-clock receive time, not the sim_time publish time. This causes misalignment on playback.

dead_ends.action: Record bag and assume timestamps match simulation time
dead_ends.why_fails: ros2 bag record uses wall clock for the bag metadata timestamps. Sim_time message headers have correct sim_time but the bag index uses wall clock.

dead_ends.action: Use --use-sim-time flag on ros2 bag record
dead_ends.why_fails: This changes the bag recorder's clock to sim_time but can cause recording gaps if clock publishes are delayed or irregular.

workarounds.action: Use --use-sim-time with a reliable clock source (e.g., Gazebo clock)
workarounds.how: ros2 bag record --use-sim-time /topic1 /topic2 /clock  # always record /clock topic

workarounds.action: Always record the /clock topic alongside other topics
workarounds.how: ros2 bag record -a --include-hidden-topics  # or explicitly: ros2 bag record /clock /cmd_vel /scan

workarounds.action: Use message header timestamps (not bag timestamps) for time-critical analysis
workarounds.how: When processing bag data, use msg.header.stamp not the bag receive timestamp for accurate timing
